candidates.why: Listed as a candidate but appears to be a specific type of income, not total revenue; better revenue line exists outside candidate list.
Candidate but represents operating profit, not top-line revenue.
Candidate but clearly financial income, not operating revenue.
Interest income is non-operating; not suitable for total revenue.
Dividend income is non-operating; not suitable for total revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Represents net income to common shareholders, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Incl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Represents net income to common shareholders including extraordinary items, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the period
candidates.why: Represents comprehensive income, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Contract Assets - Short-Term - Unbilled Revenue in Excess of Advance Consideration & Progress Billings
candidates.why: Balance sheet contract asset, not the income statement revenue flow.
Balance sheet accrued income, not total revenue.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. The proper top-line appears to be 'Revenue and other operating income', which is not in the REVT candidate list but clearly represents total revenue plus operating income. All provided REVT candidates are net income, operating income, financial income, or balance-sheet items and do not match the revenue concept. Selected the most semantically correct revenue line from the full A-column. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Revenue and other operating income

candidates.why: Generic label suggesting direct production/service costs, but all preview values are nan; may be a header or legacy line.
Clearly describes direct material and subcontracting costs; populated and consistent with COGS concept.
Personnel expenses could be part of COGS or SG&A; ambiguous without more detail.
Broad category that likely includes overhead; not clean direct cost measure.
Administrative overhead, typically SG&A rather than COGS.
Appears to be a total of all operating costs; too aggregated for pure COGS.
Financial, not operating cost of goods sold.
Financial items, not COGS.
Financial item at subsidiary level; not COGS.
Specific operating expense, typically SG&A, not direct COGS.
R&D cost, not COGS.
Inventory component on balance sheet, not the period COGS flow.
Liability, not cost flow.

variables.notes: Selected 'Materials, supplies and subcontracting' as the primary direct production cost line; it is clearly COGS-like and populated. 'Direct Costs' appears less informative and is entirely nan in the preview, so treated as less reliable.

candidates.why: Represents revenue, not SG&A overhead expenses; should not be used for XSGA.
Personnel-related operating overhead, appropriate as an SG&A component if not clearly in COGS.
Classic SG&A-type administrative overhead, even though values show as nan in preview.
Explicit SG&A bucket; appears mostly nan but is the most semantically direct SG&A line.

variables.notes: An explicit 'Selling, General & Admin' line exists (preferred SG&A bucket). Because it appears mostly empty in the preview, additional overhead components 'Salary and personnel expenses' and 'Administrative Expenses' are also included to capture SG&A across years, following the hierarchy rules. Revenue line is intentionally excluded.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: Represents amortization of capitalized R&D, not the current-period R&D expense itself; less ideal for XRD.
Explicitly labeled research & development expense; best match for XRD.

variables.notes: Used the explicit current-period R&D expense line rather than amortization of capitalized R&D.

candidates.why: Primary gross interest expense line on the income statement; best first-choice for XINT.
More specific interest expense on borrowings; good alternative if main line is missing for some years.
Net interest position; use only if gross expense lines are unavailable.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest on lease liabilities; a component of total interest expense, useful as fallback for certain years.
Represents profit attributable to non-controlling interests, not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; secondary to main reported lines.
Balance sheet principal amount of debt, not the interest expense flow.
Balance sheet liability for unpaid interest, not the period expense line.
Equity of minorities; unrelated to interest expense.
Equity of minorities; unrelated to interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: Provided a priority list of interest expense lines, preferring the main 'Interest expenses' line, then specific borrowings interest, then lease interest as additional fallback. Net interest and balance sheet items are excluded from the final choice but remain in candidates.

candidates.why: Flows within equity, not the level of book equity itself.

candidates.row_label: Non-current investments other than investments accounted for using equity method
candidates.why: An asset, not equity.
Component of equity, but not total equity attributable to owners.
Appears to represent equity attributable to owners/parent, separate from non-controlling interest; ideal for BE without NCI.
Likely total equity including non-controlling interests, as it is larger than 'Total Equity' and followed by 'Total equity and liabilities'. Use only if owner-equity line were absent.

variables.notes: Balance sheet presents 'Total Equity' followed by separate 'Non-controlling interest' and 'Minority Interest' lines, and then a larger 'Total equity'. This structure indicates 'Total Equity' is equity attributable to owners/parent, which is the correct BE measure excluding minorities.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to minorities; not the balance sheet stock of minority equity used in BE+MIB denominator.
Primary balance sheet line item for minority interests; best choice for MIB.
Duplicate labeling of the same non-controlling interest; useful as alternative label across periods.
Comprehensive income attributable to minorities; again a flow, not the stock of minority equity.

variables.notes: Used balance sheet minority equity lines as a priority list. 'Non-controlling interest' is primary, with 'Minority Interest' as an alternate label for other periods. Income statement allocations are excluded because MIB should be based on equity, not profit flows.
